fields.description:
## Why
Nothing currently checks the DIFF against its ticket. plan-review checks the plan; completion-verifier
checks at close; a diff-time scope check is complementary and catches scope drift in the change itself.

## What
New scope-intent overlay, content-triggered when >=1 ticket resolves from the commit's rebar-ticket:
trailer. Grounded prerequisites from plan review:
- REGISTER `scope-intent` in the closed OVERLAY_IDS tuple in registry.py (unknown ids are dropped).
- assemble_diff (assemble.py) does NOT currently read the rebar-ticket: trailer; add trailer parsing via
  extract_ticket_refs (supports multiple ids: multiple trailer lines AND whitespace-separated tokens) and
  assemble the referenced tickets' scope/AC as this overlay's context.
- Ticket-blindness is NOT free: ticket_context is threaded into the shared reviewer_input, so keeping base
  + other overlays ticket-blind requires explicit plumbing (gate ticket context to the scope-intent
  overlay only; do not leak it to the others). Verify with a test.
Checks the diff against the UNION of all referenced tickets so legitimate multi-ticket commits don't
produce false scope findings. Reuses plan-review's scope-intent criterion vocabulary. Advisory.

## Scope
reviewers/code-review-scope-intent.md, registry.py (OVERLAY_IDS + content trigger), assemble.py (trailer
read + ticket-context assembly), criteria_routing.json (SHARED — coordinate with C, F).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `scope-intent` in OVERLAY_IDS; overlay fires only when a ticket resolves from the trailer.
- [ ] assemble_diff reads the trailer (all ids) and assembles union scope context.
- [ ] A test proves base + other overlays receive NO ticket context (ticket-blind).
- [ ] A multi-ticket commit naming all its tickets produces no false scope-drift finding (eval).
- [ ] Overlay is advisory.
